{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the information provided, oxytocin is known to cause dangerous tachycardia, arrhythmias, and myocardial ischemia, especially when given in inappropriate dosages. Methylergometrine, misoprostol, and carboprost are less commonly associated with these severe side effects in the context of heart disease. Therefore, oxytocin should not be used to control postpartum hemorrhage (PPH) in a patient with heart disease.", "answer_choice": "B"}
